Title: Confirm CMMC scope and target level
Summary: Confirm whether each in-scope system handles only FCI or also CUI, and lock the program target at Level 2 with Level 1 as the hygiene baseline.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Scope memo approved; assessment target and in-scope environment documented in SSP.

Title: Tailor SSP with real boundary and owners
Summary: Replace placeholders in the SSP with actual system names, owners, locations, trust zones, data flows, and in-scope services.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: SSP updated with real environment details and approved for working use.

Title: Build authoritative asset inventory
Summary: Create and maintain a single inventory of endpoints, servers, SaaS platforms, network devices, and security tooling in scope for CUI.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Inventory exists, has owners, and is referenced by the SSP.

Title: Approve policy review cadence
Summary: Define policy owners, approval authority, review cycle, and document control for all CMMC artifacts.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Document register and review cadence approved; owners named.

Title: Create access control policy and procedure
Summary: Add organization-specific AC documentation covering account approval, least privilege, remote access, mobile, wireless, and portable media restrictions.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: AC policy and procedure approved and mapped to SSP practices 3.1.x.

Title: Enforce account lifecycle and least privilege
Summary: Implement joiner/mover/leaver workflow, privileged access restrictions, and periodic access reviews for all in-scope systems.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Access workflow documented; review records and role assignments available.

Title: Harden remote access paths
Summary: Restrict remote administration to approved methods with MFA, encryption, logging, and documented approval for privileged remote access.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Remote access architecture documented; MFA enforced; logs retained.

Title: Create identification and authentication procedure
Summary: Document identity proofing, unique IDs, password or authenticator requirements, MFA expectations, and service account handling.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: IA procedure approved and referenced by operations team.

Title: Validate MFA and unique identity coverage
Summary: Confirm all in-scope admin and user access uses unique identities and MFA where required by policy and system design.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Control matrix shows MFA and unique identity coverage for each system.

Title: Tailor audit policy and procedure
Summary: Update AU templates to reflect real log sources, review roles, retention expectations, and alert thresholds.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Approved AU documents reflect actual monitoring stack and process.

Title: Centralize audit logging
Summary: Ensure critical systems send security-relevant logs to a central platform with time sync, retention, and role-based access.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Central log coverage defined; retention and access settings evidenced.

Title: Establish audit review cadence
Summary: Create recurring review process for privileged actions, authentication failures, administrative changes, and security events.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Recurring review records exist and exceptions are tracked.

Title: Tailor configuration management documents
Summary: Update CM policy and procedure to define baselines, change approvals, emergency changes, and security impact analysis.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Approved CM documents define the real change path.

Title: Define secure baselines for in-scope systems
Summary: Publish baseline configurations for endpoints, servers, cloud services, identity providers, and network components.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Baseline standards exist and are mapped to asset classes.

Title: Implement formal change control
Summary: Track infrastructure and security-impacting changes in tickets with approval, testing, rollback, and evidence retention.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Sample change tickets show approval, testing, and closure evidence.

Title: Tailor maintenance policy and procedure
Summary: Define approved maintenance methods, remote maintenance safeguards, tool control, and maintenance logging.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: MA documents approved with named maintainers and logging expectations.

Title: Control privileged maintenance activity
Summary: Ensure maintenance sessions are authorized, logged, and limited to approved personnel and methods.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Maintenance records demonstrate approval and traceability.

Title: Tailor awareness and training documents
Summary: Update AT templates for role-based training, insider threat content, and frequency for admins, users, and executives.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: AT documents approved with role assignments and annual cadence.

Title: Launch CMMC-aligned training program
Summary: Deliver baseline security awareness and role-based training for privileged users, incident responders, and managers.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Training completion records and content inventory available.

Title: Tailor personnel security documents
Summary: Update PS templates for screening, onboarding, transfer, and termination actions tied to system access.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: PS documents approved and integrated with access lifecycle.

Title: Operationalize onboarding and termination controls
Summary: Ensure background screening, acceptable use acknowledgement, and timely deprovisioning are consistently executed.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Completed onboarding and termination samples show control execution.

Title: Tailor incident response procedure
Summary: Update the IR procedure to include severity definitions, reporting paths, communications, evidence handling, and lessons learned.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: IR procedure approved with contacts, triggers, and escalation path.

Title: Run incident response exercise
Summary: Test the incident workflow with a tabletop or live simulation tied to a realistic CUI-impacting scenario.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Exercise completed; actions and improvements documented.

Title: Tailor risk assessment documents
Summary: Update RA documents to define assessment cadence, scoring, ownership, treatment options, and review path.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: RA documents approved and linked to remediation workflow.

Title: Perform baseline Level 2 gap assessment
Summary: Assess implemented controls against all applicable Level 2 practices and record remediation items.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Gap register or POA&M exists with owners and dates.

Title: Tailor security assessment documents
Summary: Update CA documents to define internal review cadence, assessor support materials, and remediation verification.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: CA documents approved and linked to readiness process.

Title: Build evidence index by practice
Summary: Create a practice-by-practice evidence register tying each Level 2 requirement to artifacts, owners, and storage locations.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Evidence index covers all in-scope Level 2 practices.

Title: Create media protection policy and procedure
Summary: Document labeling, storage, transport, sanitization, backup media handling, and restrictions on portable storage for CUI.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: MP policy and procedure approved and mapped to SSP media practices.

Title: Control removable media and backups
Summary: Implement restrictions and handling procedures for removable media, backup exports, and CUI-containing artifacts.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Media handling records and technical restrictions are evidenced.

Title: Create physical protection procedure set
Summary: Document facility access, visitor escorting, logs, workstation protections, and alternate work site rules for CUI.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: PE procedure approved with site-specific controls and roles.

Title: Validate physical access controls
Summary: Verify badges, locks, visitor logs, camera coverage where used, and secure alternate work site expectations.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Physical control samples and site records are retained.

Title: Create system and communications protection standards
Summary: Document boundary protections, encryption expectations, network segmentation, session protection, and CUI transmission safeguards.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: SC standards approved and reflected in architecture documentation.

Title: Validate encryption and boundary protections
Summary: Confirm approved cryptography for CUI in transit and at rest where applicable, plus segmentation and protected administration paths.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Architecture evidence and config samples prove protections are active.

Title: Create system and information integrity procedure
Summary: Document vulnerability management, malicious code protection, flaw remediation, and security alert handling.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: SI procedure approved with scan, patch, and exception workflow.

Title: Stand up vulnerability and patch cadence
Summary: Define routine scanning, patch windows, exception handling, and verification for in-scope systems.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Scan reports, patch records, and exception tracking are available.

Title: Run internal mock assessment
Summary: Conduct a dry run against the SSP, policies, procedures, and evidence set before formal assessment activity.
Evidence / Acceptance Criteria: Mock assessment report completed; final remediation items assigned.
